title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Language Clinic: Context Cleanup
summary: A Bookbot relies on the platform default encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, translates newlines differently by platform, reads the entire file twice, and leaks a handle on failure. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the context cleanup increment in explicit text-I/O boundary. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.
Test LF, CRLF, CR, final-newline, blank-line, and universal-newline behavior without changing semantic counts accidentally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-text-io-encoding-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-text-io-encoding-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_text_io_encoding_3(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-context-cleanup-1pxwmh
# Competency: bookbot-context-cleanup.
# Case 7532: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_context_cleanup_1pxwmh(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pxwmh = "1pxwmh-hmwxp1-7532"
    assert evidence_variant_1pxwmh
    evidence_axes_1pxwmh = (
        "corpus1pxwmh",
        "policyhmwxp1",
        "revision7532",
        "command1pxwmh7532",
        "reporthmwxp17532",
        "test75321pxwmh",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pxwmh)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"

Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Context Cleanup model · Theory Context Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: The operating system will promptly close every forgotten file handle.
